prompt:
Training runs end-to-end without crashing, but the loss stays at ~7.5 for thousands of steps and the model produces gibberish after the run. The script is below. Identify the root cause and propose a fix.

Script:
```python
from datasets import load_dataset
from transformers import (AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer,
                          DataCollatorForLanguageModeling, Trainer, TrainingArguments)

model_id = 'TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0'
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id)
ds = load_dataset('wikitext', 'wikitext-2-raw-v1', split='train[:1000]')
ds = ds.map(lambda b: tokenizer(b['text'], truncation=True, max_length=512),
            batched=True, remove_columns=['text'])
collator = DataCollatorForLanguageModeling(tokenizer=tokenizer, mlm=True, mlm_probability=0.15)
args = TrainingArguments(output_dir='./out', per_device_train_batch_size=4,
                         num_train_epochs=1, learning_rate=5e-5)
trainer = Trainer(model=model, args=args, train_dataset=ds,
                  data_collator=collator, tokenizer=tokenizer)
trainer.train()
```
No traceback - training completes, but the loss is flat and outputs are garbage.

criteria.description: Identifies the actual root cause, not a downstream symptom
Suggests a fix that would actually resolve it
Does not introduce a new bug in the fix
Explains why the bug happened (mechanistic understanding)

reference_solution:
Root cause: the DataCollatorForLanguageModeling was instantiated with `mlm=True`, which masks 15% of tokens for masked-language-modeling. But TinyLlama is a CAUSAL language model (AutoModelForCausalLM). Causal LMs are trained to predict the NEXT token given previous tokens; they don't have a [MASK] head and they ignore the masking labels MLM produces. The model is effectively training on no useful signal.

Fix: pass `mlm=False`:
```python
collator = DataCollatorForLanguageModeling(tokenizer=tokenizer, mlm=False)
```
This switches the collator to produce next-token-prediction labels (labels = input_ids shifted by one, with -100 for padded positions), which is what AutoModelForCausalLM expects.

Mechanism: DataCollatorForLanguageModeling is dual-purpose. With mlm=True, it replaces 15% of tokens with [MASK] and uses those as targets - this is the BERT-style training objective. With mlm=False, it produces causal-LM labels via simple shifting. AutoModelForCausalLM's loss head computes cross-entropy on the shifted labels; it has no mechanism for [MASK]-token prediction. Loss stays at ~ln(vocab_size) because the model can only guess randomly at the masked positions.
